mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns fail to materialize in >55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals deteriorating consumer spending power and rising recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which amplifies corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or positive returns occur in <50% of co-expansion periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are non-negative in >65% of high-inflation observations, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance during CPIAUCSL > 250 periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows, or positive returns fail to materialize in >55% of industrial production expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor demand and economic resilience, reducing recession risk and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >60% of months where payroll gains exceed 150,000, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating earnings forecasts and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in multivariate regression, or co-growth periods yield negative returns in >55% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a supply-demand mismatch and economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >60% of divergence episodes, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy exceeding 52% in out-of-sample validation.
